title: santaloides minor subsp. microphylla
intro: Santaloides minor subsp. microphylla, also known as the 'Minor Sandalwood', is a subspecies of sandalwood tree native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in the tropical regions of Hawaii. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as skin and wound healing, and relief from stress and anxiety. The Minor Sandalwood is often prepared through herbal preparations like essential oil distillation, which can be used in aromatherapy, or as a dried herb for teas and infusions. The health benefits of Santaloides minor subsp. microphylla are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
